feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Ebola outbreak live updates: Flight bound for Detroit diverted to Canada after Congolese passenger boarded 'in error,' U.S. officials say
entry.summary: Meanwhile, an American doctor who was flown to Germany for treatment after contracting the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo is "critically ill," his...

entry.title: Study finds deadly hantavirus more widespread in Pacific Northwest rodents than expected
entry.summary: A hantavirus that can cause a deadly respiratory illness in humans may be more common in rodent populations in parts of the Pacific Northwest than previously re

entry.title: Ebola responders say aid cuts by Western nations left them ill-equipped for outbreak
entry.summary: The sweeping aid cuts have left the Democratic Republic of Congo struggling to contain an Ebola outbreak despite its extensive experience with the disease.

entry.title: Study Shows Meat Eaters Are More Likely to Live to 100, But There's a Catch
entry.summary: Humans are living longer than ever before.

entry.title: Scientists Finally Identified Why Mosquitoes Target Certain People Far More Than Others
entry.summary: Some people walk outside covered in bites while others beside them escape untouched. Researchers now know why, and the answer is chemical.

entry.title: Gonorrhoea and syphilis hit record levels in Europe, data shows
entry.summary: STIs have surged thanks to record cases and gaps in testing and prevention, a health agency reports.

entry.title: An Early Warning Sign of Alzheimer's May Be Keeping Some Women Up at Night
entry.summary: Catching Alzheimer's early means a better opportunity for it to be studied and for treatments to be tried.

entry.title: A Drugstore Secret for Treating Perimenopause and PMDD?
entry.summary: “Women will go to their doctors and say, ‘I take Allegra and Pepcid and my PMDD symptoms go away. It’s like magic.’’’ But does it really work, and why?

entry.title: Marathons and ultramarathons may be linked to colon cancer. Here’s why.
entry.summary: A small study found a surprisingly high incidence of precancerous polyps in young, extreme runners, sparking controversy and concern.

entry.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
entry.summary: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

entry.title: What Ebola and hantavirus are revealing about America’s public health system
entry.summary: Public health experts say neither virus poses an immediate threat to most Americans. But the trust deficit the viruses are exposing could be catastrophic when crisis arrives.

entry.title: Researchers Discover the Body’s Hidden “Off Switch” for Inflammation
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered a previously underexplored mechanism that may act as one of the body’s natural “off switches” for inflammation.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Metformin Doesn’t Work the Way We Thought
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered evidence that metformin’s effects may begin in an unexpected place: the intestine. For years, researchers believed metformin, the most widely used medication for type 2 diabetes, worked mainly by reducing glucose production in the li…

entry.title: An Early Clue to Alzheimer's May Appear as Young as 45, Study Finds
entry.summary: Alzheimer's disease, a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions worldwide, has a long preclinical stage.

entry.title: These Popular Supplements Are Sold With Anti-Aging Claims. Here's What Science Says.
entry.summary: As more people look for ways to stay younger for longer, the supplement industry has moved beyond creams and cosmetic fixes to something more ambitious: products that claim to slow aging by acting on cellular processes.

entry.title: 4 Things to Do Before 10 A.M. to Reduce Dementia Risk
entry.summary: Some morning habits may help reduce dementia risk. Learn what brain experts recommend doing before 10 a.m. for cognitive health.

entry.title: Depression appears to alter how young adults remember childhood trauma and adversity
entry.summary: A new longitudinal study reveals that depression can alter how young adults remember childhood trauma. When depressive symptoms worsen, individuals are more likely to recall past adversity, pointing to current emotional state as a major driver of autobiograph…

entry.title: US Enlists Small Biotech for Experimental Ebola Treatment
entry.summary: US officials are working with a small biotech company to provide an experimental treatment that may be used in patients exposed to Ebola, according to a person familiar with the matter.

entry.title: High blood pressure, heart attacks linked to common preservatives in food
entry.summary: Common “natural” preservatives such as vitamin C may be linked to a greater risk of high blood pressure and heart disease, a new study found.

entry.title: US taps small San Diego biotech for experimental Ebola treatment as epidemic worsens
entry.summary: As an outbreak of Ebola in central Africa continues to worsen, the federal government has tapped a little-known biotech in San Diego to provide doses of an experimental antibody that may be used to | As an outbreak of Ebola in central Africa continues to wors…

entry.title: Common food preservatives linked to high blood pressure and heart disease
entry.summary: Eating foods that contain common preservative food additives may increase the risks of high blood pressure and cardiovascular disease, according to research published in the European Heart Journal.

entry.title: Spinal neuromotor rehabilitation using a portable isokinetic training robot
entry.summary: Nature - Spinal neuromotor rehabilitation using a portable isokinetic training robot

entry.title: 3 Foods With More Vitamin D Than Salmon
entry.summary: Meeting your vitamin D needs with whole foods can be a challenge, especially if you are not a fan of salmon. Here are some other options that can fill in the nutrition gap.

entry.title: White House resisted letting doctor with Ebola return to U.S.
entry.summary: The administration’s reluctance to bring an infected American back to U.S. soil marked a sharp contrast with the 2014 West Africa Ebola outbreak.

entry.title: Tea or Coffee? Your Daily Choice Could Affect Osteoporosis Risk
entry.summary: A large osteoporosis study found that tea and coffee may affect aging bones differently.

entry.title: A pathogen lncRNA secreted into rice sequesters a host miRNA for virulence
entry.summary: A fungal long non-coding RNA from Magnaporthe oryzae translocates into rice cells to sequester a host microRNA that normally represses PKR1, a negative immunity regulator, thereby facilitating infection and revealing a widespread RNA-based pathogen–host …

entry.title: Genetic analysis of circulating metabolic traits in 619,372 individuals
entry.summary: A genome-wide association study combining data from the Estonian Biobank and the UK Biobank identifies many common and low-frequency locus–metabolic trait associations, enabling the identification of putative causal links with disease outcomes.

entry.title: Dopamine drives persistent remodelling of the maternal brain
entry.summary: Brain-wide transcriptomic profiling in mice reveals that reproductive experience remodels the maternal brain by altering dopamine dynamics in the dorsal hippocampal formation, causing dopamine-dependent histone post-translational modifications, and thereby ch…

entry.title: Neural representation of action symbols in primate frontal cortex
entry.summary: A drawing-like task designed to study compositional generalization identifies a specific neural population in the ventral premotor cortex in primates that encodes action symbols.

entry.title: A deep-learning framework reveals whole-body perturbations at cell level
entry.summary: An analysis of diet-induced obesity using MouseMapper—a suite of foundation-model-based deep-learning algorithms—identifies structural alterations of the infraorbital branch of the trigeminal ganglia.

entry.title: Vitamin C May Fight Cancer in a Surprising Way
entry.summary: Intravenous vitamin C may help fight cancer and reduce treatment side effects, though it remains experimental. Linus Pauling’s theory was flawed but partly correct.

entry.title: Too little or too much sleep is linked to faster ageing throughout the body
entry.summary: A multimodal analysis reveals a ‘U-shaped’ association between sleep duration and biological ageing across various organ systems, with too much or not enough sleep being linked with accelerated organ ageing. The study also shows that biological ageing differe…

entry.title: These are the best knee braces for arthritis, according to orthopedic surgeons
entry.summary: I spoke with orthopedic surgeons and doctors to find the best knee braces for arthritis that diminish joint pain and support movement.

entry.title: AI atlas reveals hidden whole-body-damage caused by obesity
entry.summary: Obesity affects far more than metabolism and fat storage. It alters immune activity, nerve structure, and tissue organization across multiple organ systems, increasing the risk of diseases including type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, stroke, neuropathy …

entry.title: Russia Uncovered 511 Billion Barrels of Oil in Antarctica: A Find That Could Turn the Frozen Continent Into a Flashpoint
entry.summary: Russian ships have mapped oil reserves in Antarctica’s Weddell Sea that nearly double Saudi Arabia’s.

entry.title: Vegan College Student Dies by Suicide. Experts Say Her Mental Decline Was a 'Direct Result' of Her Diet
entry.summary: An investigation into the death of Georgina Owen found that her mental health declined after she stopped taking vitamin B12 supplements

entry.title: Younger partners and sex toy use are associated with less severe symptoms of menopause
entry.summary: New research finds that using sex toys and dating younger partners are associated with less severe menopause symptoms. The study highlights how diverse sexual behaviors and relationship styles intertwine with physical and emotional well-being during this tran…

entry.title: Neurosurgeons Are Weirdly Optimistic About Cryonics for Life Extension, Survey Finds
entry.summary: Researchers surveyed over 300 physicians to get their take on leaving patients in suspended animation for future revival.

entry.title: 25 Bizarre Discoveries By Scientists That Prove We Almost Understand Nothing About The Universe
entry.summary: "The disturbing part is that oxygen was actually toxic to most life at the time."

entry.title: Common asthma drug helps fight hard-to-treat cancers, including aggressive breast cancers, early study finds
entry.summary: Scientists found that blocking a protein best known for its role in asthma enhances cancer immunotherapy in preclinical models.

entry.title: The expert’s guide to vitamin B12 deficiency
entry.summary: The dangers of having low levels of the vitamin were laid bare this week after a university student died. Here’s what you need to know

entry.title: Why a deadly, massive measles outbreak in Bangladesh has some U.S. health experts concerned
entry.summary: A CBS News medical correspondent and doctor says her "biggest concern for the World Cup is actually measles. It's not hantavirus, it is not Ebola."

entry.title: A rare Ebola strain is spreading with no vaccine. Here's what you need to know
entry.summary: This outbreak is being called "the perfect storm." How did it start, what are the characteristics of the strain that's causing it and how much of a threat is it to global health?

entry.title: Philadelphia Department of Public Health Notifying Patients of Smiles at Rittenhouse Square Dental Clinic of Risk of Exposure
entry.summary: Philadelphia Department of Public Health Notifying Patients of Smiles at Rittenhouse Square Dental Clinic of Risk of Exposure

entry.title: Column | Why ‘non-ultra-processed’ food labels won’t make Americans eat better
entry.summary: Food manufacturers want us to buy more food. New labels won’t change their motives or our habits.

entry.title: Ebola and hantavirus reveal US as a major threat to global health
entry.summary: America didn't cause these diseases. But Trump’s cuts to public health have put the world at risk

entry.title: Are Dates Healthier Than Sugar? What Dietitians Say
entry.summary: Dates are a more natural alternative to regular sugar. Discover what makes them a slightly healthier choice.

entry.title: The 8 Best Mediterranean Diet Foods to Buy at Costco, According to a Food Writer
entry.summary: Want to embrace the Mediterranean lifestyle on a budget? Head to your local Costco with this food-writer approved shopping list.

entry.title: Study shows over half of Wisconsin deer ticks carry Lyme disease-causing bacteria
entry.summary: MADISON, Wis. — More than half of deer ticks tested positive for carrying the bacteria that causes Lyme disease, according to a study conducted in Wisconsin.

entry.title: Your Daily Rhythms May Help Slow Biological Aging, Study Suggests
entry.summary: We're all a certain number of years and months and days old, but alongside this chronological age is a biological age: the rate at which our bodies are wearing down.
